name: LibreNMS Network Monitoring API
what_it_does: Open-source network monitoring and management platform with REST API — auto-discovering network devices via SNMP, monitoring ports, bandwidth, CPU, memory, and services with comprehensive alerting, visualization, and API for device management and metric access.
use_cases: Querying network device health and interface statistics via REST API
Managing device inventory and SNMP credentials programmatically via API
Fetching alert history and current alarm states for NOC dashboards
Creating custom alert rules and notification channels via API
Integrating LibreNMS network data with ITSM or ticketing systems
not_for: Application performance monitoring (use Datadog or Dynatrace for APM)
Log management (use ELK Stack or Graylog for logs)
Cloud-native Kubernetes monitoring without physical network context
best_when: An operations team needs open-source network monitoring for physical/virtual infrastructure with SNMP-based device discovery, bandwidth graphs, and API integration for NOC tooling.
avoid_when: You need APM, log management, or cloud-native container monitoring without physical network devices.

auth.notes: API token generated in LibreNMS UI. X-Auth-Token header. Self-hosted deployment — no external auth. User-level token inherits user permissions. Admin token for full API access. No OAuth2 or scope granularity.

pricing.notes: 100% open-source (GPL v3). Community-supported. Fork of Observium. Self-hosted only — no commercial SaaS. Auto-discovery via SNMP, CDP, LLDP. 1,000+ device types supported. Global network operations center (NOC) community. Active development.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for devices, ports, alerts, graphs, and inventory. API token auth. No webhooks — polling required for alerts. Good documentation. Self-hosted means endpoint varies. PHP-based platform. API coverage is comprehensive for device and network data.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device reads and metric queries fully idempotent. Device addition idempotent by hostname. Alert acknowledgment idempotent. Graph data queries idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted deployment — no standard API endpoint
No webhooks — must poll for alert state changes
SNMP credentials must be configured before devices can be monitored
RRD database for historical graphs — data retention limited by RRD configuration

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable (web server dependent). API token auth — no scope granularity. SNMP community strings sensitive — must be protected. Self-hosted data control. GPL v3 license. Network device credentials stored locally — encrypt at rest. Standard network monitoring security practices.
